evals.prompt: I want to add MatrixScan Count to my .NET MAUI app. Here's my empty page: EmptyBarcodeCountPage.xaml + EmptyBarcodeCountPage.xaml.cs, and my MauiProgram: EmptyMauiProgram.cs. I need to count EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes, show the built-in counting UI, and when the user taps the List button store the scanned barcodes so I can show them on a results screen.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, fetches the latest Scandit MAUI NuGet versions, updates MauiProgram.cs with .UseScanditCore().UseScanditBarcode(c => c.AddBarcodeCountView()), adds <scandit:BarcodeCountView> with DataCaptureContext and BarcodeCount bindings (and ViewStyle) to the page using the Count.UI.Maui XAML namespace, writes a complete BarcodeCount integration (DataCaptureContext.ForLicenseKey, a manually-managed Camera via Camera.GetDefaultCamera(BarcodeCount.RecommendedCameraSettings) + SetFrameSourceAsync, new BarcodeCountSettings() enabling only Ean13Upca and Code128, BarcodeCount.Create(context, settings), the Scanned event copying session.RecognizedBarcodes out via MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread, ListButtonTapped subscribed in HandlerChanged storing the barcodes, camera SwitchToDesiredStateAsync on/off + barcodeCount.Enabled across OnAppearing/OnDisappearing). Shows the setup checklist (four NuGet packages, NSCameraUsageDescription on iOS, SupportedOSPlatformVersion 24 on Android, license key). Does NOT add ScanditCaptureCore.Initialize() calls.

assertions.text: Setup checklist mentions all four NuGet packages: Scandit.DataCapture.Core, Scandit.DataCapture.Core.Maui, Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode, Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Maui
MauiProgram.cs is updated to call .UseScanditCore() with NO inner configure lambda
MauiProgram.cs calls .UseScanditBarcode( with an inner configure lambda that invokes AddBarcodeCountView()
MauiProgram.cs does NOT call .UseScanditCore(c => c.AddDataCaptureView()) (that is the BarcodeBatch/BarcodeCapture chain, wrong for BarcodeCount)
No ScanditCaptureCore.Initialize() or ScanditBarcodeCapture.Initialize() call is added to MainApplication or AppDelegate (the MAUI builder handles this)
The page XAML adds the xmlns:scandit="clr-namespace:Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Count.UI.Maui;assembly=ScanditBarcodeCaptureMaui" namespace
The page XAML contains a <scandit:BarcodeCountView> element
<scandit:BarcodeCountView> sets DataCaptureContext="{Binding DataCaptureContext}" (binding to a DataCaptureContext property)
<scandit:BarcodeCountView> sets BarcodeCount="{Binding BarcodeCount}" (binding to a BarcodeCount property)
The view style is set via the ViewStyle property (e.g. ViewStyle="Icon" or ViewStyle="Dot"), NOT via a Style attribute
Setup checklist mentions adding NSCameraUsageDescription to Platforms/iOS/Info.plist
Setup checklist mentions SupportedOSPlatformVersion of at least 24 for the Android target
A license key placeholder string is present
DataCaptureContext.ForLicenseKey( is used to create the context
new BarcodeCountSettings() is used (the settings use a plain constructor)
BarcodeCount.Create( is used as the factory (NOT new BarcodeCount(...), which has no public constructor)
Symbology.Ean13Upca is enabled
Symbology.Code128 is enabled
No symbologies other than Ean13Upca and Code128 are enabled
Kotlin-style underscore symbology names (Symbology.EAN13_UPCA, Symbology.CODE_128) are NOT used, and Swift-style camelCase (.ean13UPCA) is NOT used
A Camera is obtained via Camera.GetDefaultCamera(...) using BarcodeCount.RecommendedCameraSettings
dataCaptureContext.SetFrameSourceAsync( is called with the camera (the camera is managed explicitly, not by the view)
camera.SwitchToDesiredStateAsync(FrameSourceState.On) and FrameSourceState.Off are used to drive the camera across the lifecycle
barcodeCount.Enabled is set to true so frames are processed
No barcodeCountView.OnAppearing()/OnDisappearing()/StartScanning() calls are made (those are SparkScanView methods; the MAUI BarcodeCountView does not have them)
Either barcodeCount.Scanned += handler or barcodeCount.AddListener(IBarcodeCountListener) is wired
The scan handler reads args.Session.RecognizedBarcodes (the BarcodeCountSession exposes RecognizedBarcodes, not AddedTrackedBarcodes)

The recognized barcodes are copied out of the session (e.g. .ToList()) inside the callback, and UI work is dispatched via MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread (not RunOnUiThread, not DispatchQueue.MainQueue)
The ListButtonTapped (and/or ExitButtonTapped) event is subscribed inside the barcodeCountView.HandlerChanged handler, not in the constructor before the handler exists
The ListButtonTapped handler stores/shows the scanned barcodes
OnAppearing is overridden and triggers a flow that calls Permissions.CheckStatusAsync<Permissions.Camera>() or Permissions.RequestAsync<Permissions.Camera>()
No Android-only APIs appear (RunOnUiThread, FindViewById, FrameLayout, AppCompatActivity, container.AddView, [Activity])
No BarcodeArView, IBarcodeArHighlightProvider, or BarcodeAr API is used (MatrixScan Count is not MatrixScan AR)
No generic <scandit:DataCaptureView> or BarcodeCaptureOverlay is created (BarcodeCount uses its own <scandit:BarcodeCountView>)

evals.prompt: I'm building a receiving app with MatrixScan Count on .NET MAUI. I already have it counting (see IntegratedBarcodeCountPageViewModel.cs). Now I want to check scanned barcodes against an expected list: 3x of 0123456789012 and 1x of 9876543210987, and react when everything on the list has been scanned.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds a capture list: builds TargetBarcode.Create(data, quantity) entries, creates a BarcodeCountCaptureList.Create(listener, targets), implements IBarcodeCountCaptureListListener (OnObservationStarted/OnObservationStopped/OnCaptureListSessionUpdated/OnCaptureListCompleted), and applies it via barcodeCount.SetBarcodeCountCaptureList(list), preserving existing integration.

assertions.text: TargetBarcode.Create(data, quantity) is used to build the expected barcodes (factory, not new TargetBarcode(...))
A TargetBarcode for 0123456789012 with quantity 3 and one for 9876543210987 with quantity 1 are created
BarcodeCountCaptureList.Create(listener, targets) is used (factory, not new BarcodeCountCaptureList(...))
IBarcodeCountCaptureListListener is implemented with OnObservationStarted, OnObservationStopped, OnCaptureListSessionUpdated, and OnCaptureListCompleted
barcodeCount.SetBarcodeCountCaptureList(list) is called to apply the list
OnCaptureListCompleted is where the all-scanned reaction is handled
Existing integration code (camera, BarcodeCount.Create, Scanned handler, ResumeAsync/SleepAsync) is preserved
No Android- or iOS-only APIs are introduced (RunOnUiThread, DispatchQueue.MainQueue, FindViewById)

evals.prompt: I want to suppress the default beep and vibration in my MatrixScan Count .NET MAUI app — silent counting. Update the feedback in IntegratedBarcodeCountPageViewModel.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets barcodeCount.Feedback to a new BarcodeCountFeedback() (empty constructor = silent), imported from Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Count.Feedback, preserving the rest of the integration. It does NOT call DefaultFeedback as a method.

assertions.text: barcodeCount.Feedback is reassigned to a new BarcodeCountFeedback() (empty constructor)
BarcodeCountFeedback is imported from Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Count.Feedback
BarcodeCountFeedback.DefaultFeedback is NOT called as a method (with parentheses) — it is a static property
The feedback slots are referred to as Success / Failure if mentioned (NOT Scanned / Tapped, which are MatrixScan AR's)
Existing integration code (camera, BarcodeCount.Create, Scanned handler, ResumeAsync/SleepAsync) is preserved

evals.prompt: My labels carry both Code 128 and PDF417, but in my MatrixScan Count .NET MAUI app I only want to count the Code 128 ones and ignore PDF417. Update IntegratedBarcodeCountPageViewModel.cs to filter out PDF417.
evals.expected_output: The skill configures filtering on the existing BarcodeCountSettings via settings.FilterSettings.ExcludedSymbologies = new[] { Symbology.Pdf417 } (mutating the get-only FilterSettings, not reassigning it, and not using a BarcodeFilterSettings.Create() factory), preserving the rest of the integration.

assertions.text: Filtering is configured through the FilterSettings property on the BarcodeCountSettings
ExcludedSymbologies is assigned a collection containing Symbology.Pdf417
The FilterSettings property is mutated in place and is NOT reassigned to a new value (semantic)
BarcodeFilterSettings.Create() is NOT used (on .NET BarcodeFilterSettings is not created via a Create factory here)
Existing integration code (camera, BarcodeCount.Create, Scanned handler, ResumeAsync/SleepAsync) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Count .NET MAUI app I want to exclude any barcode whose data starts with 1234. Update IntegratedBarcodeCountPageViewModel.cs to filter those out with a regex.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.FilterSettings.ExcludedCodesRegex = "^1234.*" on the existing BarcodeCountSettings, preserving the rest of the integration.

assertions.text: ExcludedCodesRegex is set on the FilterSettings of the BarcodeCountSettings
The regex value targets barcodes starting with 1234 (semantic)
Existing integration code (camera, BarcodeCount.Create, Scanned handler, ResumeAsync/SleepAsync) is preserved

evals.prompt: Each physical box in my warehouse has a unique barcode value — no duplicates ever. Optimize my MatrixScan Count .NET MAUI app for that. Update IntegratedBarcodeCountPageViewModel.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets barcodeCountSettings.ExpectsOnlyUniqueBarcodes = true on the existing settings, preserving the rest of the integration.

assertions.text: ExpectsOnlyUniqueBarcodes is set to true on the BarcodeCountSettings
Existing integration code (camera, BarcodeCount.Create, Scanned handler, ResumeAsync/SleepAsync) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want my MatrixScan Count .NET MAUI app to keep the count when it goes to the background and clear it for a brand-new scanning session. Update IntegratedBarcodeCountPageViewModel.cs to seed previously-counted barcodes as additional barcodes, and add a method that starts a fresh count.
evals.expected_output: The skill calls barcodeCount.SetAdditionalBarcodes(savedBarcodes) to seed already-counted barcodes, and a reset path that calls barcodeCount.ClearAdditionalBarcodes() and barcodeCount.Reset(), preserving the existing integration.

assertions.text: barcodeCount.SetAdditionalBarcodes is called with a list of barcodes to seed already-counted barcodes
barcodeCount.ClearAdditionalBarcodes() is called to start a fresh count
barcodeCount.Reset() is called to clear the counted barcodes for a new session
Existing integration code (camera, BarcodeCount.Create, Scanned handler, ResumeAsync/SleepAsync) is preserved

evals.prompt: I have a hardware scan button on my rugged device. Enable the hardware trigger in my MatrixScan Count .NET MAUI app for both Android and iOS. Update IntegratedBarcodeCountPage.xaml.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill enables the hardware trigger from code-behind once the handler is ready, guarding the platform-specific APIs: on Android EnableHardwareTrigger (with HardwareTriggerSupported) inside an #if ANDROID block, on iOS HardwareTriggerEnabled = true inside an #elif IOS / #if IOS block, preserving the existing integration.

assertions.text: On Android the hardware trigger is enabled via barcodeCountView.EnableHardwareTrigger
On iOS the hardware trigger is enabled via barcodeCountView.HardwareTriggerEnabled = true
The Android-only and iOS-only APIs are guarded by platform conditionals (#if ANDROID / #if IOS) so each is only referenced on its own target (semantic)
HardwareTriggerEnabled is NOT used on the Android path and EnableHardwareTrigger is NOT used on the iOS path (semantic)
The hardware trigger is set up after the handler is ready (e.g. inside OnBarcodeCountViewHandlerChanged) rather than before the handler exists
Existing integration code (HandlerChanged wiring, ListButtonTapped, ExitButtonTapped, OnAppearing, OnDisappearing) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to color the recognized-barcode overlays in my MatrixScan Count .NET MAUI app and react when the user taps one. Update IntegratedBarcodeCountPage.xaml.cs to add a view listener.
evals.expected_output: The skill implements IBarcodeCountViewListener (BrushForRecognizedBarcode and the other brush-for callbacks returning a Brush?, plus the OnRecognizedBarcodeTapped / tap callbacks taking a TrackedBarcode) and assigns it to barcodeCountView.Listener inside the HandlerChanged handler, preserving the existing integration.

assertions.text: A class implementing IBarcodeCountViewListener is provided
BrushForRecognizedBarcode is implemented and returns a Brush
OnRecognizedBarcodeTapped is implemented and receives a TrackedBarcode
The listener is assigned to barcodeCountView.Listener
The listener is assigned after the handler is ready (e.g. inside OnBarcodeCountViewHandlerChanged) rather than before the handler exists
TrackedBarcode is imported from Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Batch.Data
Existing integration code (HandlerChanged wiring, ListButtonTapped, ExitButtonTapped, OnAppearing, OnDisappearing) is preserved

evals.prompt: My MatrixScan Count .NET MAUI app uses a capture list. When the user scans a barcode that is not on the list, I want to prompt them to accept or reject it. Update IntegratedBarcodeCountPage.xaml.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads barcodeCountView.BarcodeNotInListActionSettings once the handler is ready, sets Enabled = true and the Accept/Reject button text and accepted/rejected hints, preserving the existing integration.

assertions.text: barcodeCountView.BarcodeNotInListActionSettings is read to configure the not-in-list action
The not-in-list action is enabled via its Enabled property set to true
The AcceptButtonText and RejectButtonText are set
The not-in-list action is configured after the handler is ready (e.g. inside OnBarcodeCountViewHandlerChanged) rather than before the handler exists
Existing integration code (HandlerChanged wiring, ListButtonTapped, ExitButtonTapped, OnAppearing, OnDisappearing) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want a Status Mode button in my MatrixScan Count .NET MAUI app that marks every counted item as low stock. Update IntegratedBarcodeCountPage.xaml.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill implements IBarcodeCountStatusProvider (OnStatusRequested building BarcodeCountStatusItem.Create entries with BarcodeCountStatus.LowStock and calling callback.OnStatusReady with BarcodeCountStatusResultSuccess.Create), shows the status button via barcodeCountView.ShouldShowStatusModeButton = true, and registers the provider via barcodeCountView.SetStatusProvider once the handler is ready, preserving the existing integration.

assertions.text: A class implementing IBarcodeCountStatusProvider is provided with an OnStatusRequested method
BarcodeCountStatusItem.Create is used to build status items with BarcodeCountStatus.LowStock
callback.OnStatusReady is called with a BarcodeCountStatusResultSuccess.Create result
barcodeCountView.ShouldShowStatusModeButton is set to true
barcodeCountView.SetStatusProvider is called to register the provider
The status provider is registered after the handler is ready (e.g. inside OnBarcodeCountViewHandlerChanged) rather than before the handler exists
Existing integration code (HandlerChanged wiring, ListButtonTapped, ExitButtonTapped, OnAppearing, OnDisappearing) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to hide the List button, the Exit button, and the on-screen hints in my MatrixScan Count .NET MAUI app — keep just the shutter. Update IntegratedBarcodeCountPage.xaml.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets the relevant ShouldShow* properties to false on barcodeCountView (ShouldShowListButton, ShouldShowExitButton, ShouldShowHints), preserving the existing integration.

assertions.text: barcodeCountView.ShouldShowListButton is set to false
barcodeCountView.ShouldShowExitButton is set to false
barcodeCountView.ShouldShowHints is set to false
Existing integration code (HandlerChanged wiring, ListButtonTapped, ExitButtonTapped, OnAppearing, OnDisappearing) is preserved
